name: transportation_policy_licensing_backlog_millhaven_lib_eq
premise: A Transportation Policy response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving transportation policy licensing backlog millhaven lib eq. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before markets open. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as liberty vs equality under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Ensure a usable human override/appeals path. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Mei Park
agents.identity: I am Mei Park, accountable for a defensible call in Transportation Policy when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays equality for liberty.

agents.name: Omar Dubois
agents.identity: I am Omar Dubois, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Transportation Policy.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so equality isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of liberty.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect equality.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to equality to secure liberty under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before markets open. Evidence is partial. Mei Park must decide under liberty vs equality. What does Mei Park do?
